points.text: Measure per‑endpoint request rate, p95 latency, error rate, and CPU/memory usage.
Track queue depth and thread‑pool saturation to detect back‑pressure.
Set client timeout ~1.5× current p95 latency with an absolute upper bound.
Limit retries to 2‑3 attempts with exponential backoff starting at 100‑200 ms.
Use a circuit breaker that opens when error rate >5% over a 30‑second window.
Reserve 30‑40% headroom in capacity (CPU, connections) to handle a 3× traffic surge.
Measure incoming request rate (RPS) and its 95th percentile burst to size thread pools and socket buffers.
Track p95 latency per endpoint and correlate with CPU, memory, and queue length to spot saturation early.
Monitor TCP retransmission count and SYN backlog; high values indicate network congestion or insufficient listen queue.
Set a circuit‑breaker threshold at ~80% of observed max concurrent connections to stop new traffic before overload.
Limit client retries: max 2 retries with exponential backoff, and enforce a minimum backoff of 100 ms.
Configure connection timeout to 2 s and read timeout to 5 s; adjust down if p95 latency budget is <200 ms.
Enable keep‑alive with idle timeout ~30 s to recycle idle sockets and avoid resource exhaustion.
